title: Failure to build image in 0.10-2
body:
CentOS 7.4

```
$ runc --version
runc version 1.0.0-rc4+dev
commit: a031838b23e9990f67a3b4c0e89bf3d935d1298b-dirty
spec: 1.0.0
$ buildah version
Version:       0.10
Go Version:    go1.8.3
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    129fb10
Built:         Tue Dec 26 12:37:51 2017
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

Attempted to build https://github.com/openshift/release projects/origin-release/base with buildah bud, got this repeatable failure when trying to commit

```
STEP 5: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage]@151c26a38f49601ec2cc77514f47d79a0166f1512b93cd64a5d667e6a29b1d4a
error building: error copying layer and metadata: error committing new image "containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage]@151c26a38f49601ec2cc77514f47d79a0166f1512b93cd64a5d667e6a29b1d4a": error determining uncompressed digest for blob "sha256:e9c4879b5b6b53531b4585f3c5d0bb54784b77560612a1c186eb7af94d9a14dc"
```

Other containers were able to commit.  What debugging info can I provide (is 100% reproducible)

comments.body:
Can you attach the full output from 'buildah images' followed by 'buildah --debug bud ...'?  That'll tell us whether blob `sha256:e9c4879b5b6b53531b4585f3c5d0bb54784b77560612a1c186eb7af94d9a14dc` was downloaded and stored by buildah, or by something else that ran before it.
If it is a layer that was pulled down by a different package which was built using older versions of the storage or image libraries, that information may have not been recorded.  In that case, it'd be worth checking if removing the local copy of the `centos:7` image and re-trying, forcing buildah to pull down the "from" image, allows it to succeed.

Just remove from /var/run/containers/storage and
/var/lib/containers/storage be cleaned up?

On Wed, Jan 3, 2018 at 12:09 PM, Nalin Dahyabhai <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> Can you attach the full output from 'buildah images' followed by 'buildah
> --debug bud ...'? That'll tell us whether blob sha256:
> e9c4879b5b6b53531b4585f3c5d0bb54784b77560612a1c186eb7af94d9a14dc was
> downloaded and stored by buildah, or by something else that ran before it.
> If it is a layer that was pulled down by a different package which was
> built using older versions of the storage or image libraries, that
> information may have not been recorded. In that case, it'd be worth
> checking if removing the local copy of the centos:7 image and re-trying,
> forcing buildah to pull down the "from" image, allows it to succeed.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues/374#issuecomment-355067450>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/ABG_p5kBGDbBkhTlYGNcFPKpKK-ya8l0ks5tG7Q3gaJpZM4RRJGJ>
> .
>
If no container filesystems are mounted up, that's fine.  Alternately, something like `buildah containers -q | xargs buildah rm` followed by `buildah images -q | xargs buildah rmi` would also work.

We've added flags -a to `podman rm -a` and `podman rmi -a`, we should add similar to buildah, since this is a common enough command sequence.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Could you take a quick look at doing this?
